display.purpose: Hero and top-of-page statements only. Ceiling 82px.

label.purpose: IBM Plex Mono, uppercase, tracked. Annotation only — never prose.

shadows.name: ambient-raised
shadows.purpose: Product shots and default lifted panels.

shadows.name: ambient-hover
shadows.purpose: Interactive lift on cards and figures (deepens to 0 24px 60px rgba(15,23,42,0.18)).

shadows.name: amber-glow
shadows.purpose: Signal only — live progress bars, active markers, CTA edge-light. Never a depth cue.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions and hover lifts.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Content is always visible; animate only already-visible elements. Provide instant/crossfade fallback.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single amber CTA. Trailing arrow slides on hover.
components.html: Book a demo
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; background: #E8901C; color: #fff; font: 600 16px/1 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 17px 28px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid transparent; box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.18); text-decoration: none; transition: background 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #C77713; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #E8901C; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary .ds-arr { transition: transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover .ds-arr { transform: translateX(4px); }

components.name: Ghost Button (on ink)
components.description: Secondary action on the dark chrome. Hairline border brightens on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost-ink { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; background: transparent; color: #EEF2FA; font: 600 15px/1 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 14px 22px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid rgba(151,166,192,0.26); text-decoration: none; transition: border-color 160ms, background 160ms; } .ds-btn-ghost-ink:hover { border-color: #97A6C0; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.03); } .ds-btn-ghost-ink:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #E8901C; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Dark Input Field
components.description: Demo-form field on the dark chrome. Mono label, amber focus border, AA-safe placeholder.
components.css: .ds-field { display: block; } .ds-field-label { display: block; font: 500 12px/1 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #97A6C0; margin-bottom: 9px; } .ds-field-input { width: 100%; background: #0A1020; border: 1px solid rgba(151,166,192,0.26); border-radius: 8px; padding: 13px 14px; color: #EEF2FA; font: 400 15px/1.4 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; transition: border-color 140ms; } .ds-field-input::placeholder { color: #8290AA; } .ds-field-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #E8901C; }

components.name: Light Feature Card
components.description: Card on a light reading section. Flat at rest, lifts on hover.
components.html:
Price from your own history

Reuse rates from past jobs so every line item is grounded in what work actually cost you.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #fff; border: 1px solid #E5E7EB; border-radius: 12px; padding: clamp(22px,3vw,38px); box-shadow: 0 2px 10px rgba(15,23,42,0.06); transition: box-shadow 260ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), border-color 260ms, transform 260ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); border-color: #D1D5DB; box-shadow: 0 14px 34px rgba(15,23,42,0.12); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 8px; font: 600 clamp(22px,2.6vw,32px)/1.12 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: -0.015em; color: #0F172A; } .ds-card-body { margin: 0; font: 400 16px/1.6 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; color: #475569; max-width: 68ch; }

components.name: Section Eyebrow
components.description: Mono amber uppercase kicker with a lit dot. Use sparingly — not above every section.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; } .ds-eyebrow-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: #E8901C; box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(232,144,28,0.18); } .ds-eyebrow-text { font: 500 12px/1.4 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.18em; color: #E8901C; }

components.name: Estimate HUD
components.description: Signature object: a floating panel that shows a live estimate. The system's thesis — show the product, don't claim it.
components.html:
✦Gradvera · estimating offer
€1.24M
Estimate ready✦ 87% confidence
components.css: .ds-hud { background: #141E33; border: 1px solid rgba(151,166,192,0.14); border-radius: 12px; padding: 18px 20px; color: #EEF2FA; box-shadow: 0 18px 44px rgba(0,0,0,0.34); font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; max-width: 320px; } .ds-hud-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; } .ds-hud-spark { color: #E8901C; } .ds-hud-label { font: 500 11.5px/1 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: #97A6C0; } .ds-hud-val { margin: 12px 0 14px; font: 600 40px/1 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: -0.02em; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums lining-nums; } .ds-hud-cur { color: #97A6C0; margin-right: 4px; } .ds-hud-m { color: #97A6C0; font-size: 24px; margin-left: 2px; } .ds-hud-bar { height: 6px; border-radius: 3px; background: rgba(151,166,192,0.14); overflow: hidden; } .ds-hud-bar span { display: block; height: 100%; width: 87%; background: #E8901C; box-shadow: 0 0 10px rgba(232,144,28,0.6); } .ds-hud-foot { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; margin-top: 12px; font: 500 11.5px/1 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #97A6C0; } .ds-hud-conf { color: #E8901C; }

narrative.overview: Gradvera is a dark drafting table seen under a single work lamp. The ground is deep blueprint navy; one burnished-amber light picks out exactly what matters — a number, a confidence score, a call to book a demo — and nothing competes with it. Around that light, the interface annotates itself in IBM Plex Mono, the way a quantity surveyor marks up a drawing: small, precise, uppercase callouts that report rather than sell. The voice is calm, precise, and anti-hype: sentence-case headings, no exclamation marks, no superlatives. Credibility is shown, not claimed — the hero renders a live estimate (€1.24M · 87% confidence) instead of asserting accuracy in adjectives. This system explicitly rejects the generic SaaS template and sterile corporate blue; warmth and trust come from the amber accent, the technical type voice, and real product visuals, never from decoration.
keyCharacteristics: Dark blueprint-navy chrome (hero, header, footer, forms); light slate-on-white for the reading body between.
A single accent — burnished amber #E8901C — used as a work-light, sparingly.
Two IBM Plex faces: Sans for everything readable, Mono for technical annotation only.
Precision motifs (blueprint wireframe, estimate HUD) that demonstrate the product.
Content is always visible; motion animates only already-visible elements (reduced-motion safe).

rules.name: The One Light Rule
rules.body: Amber is the only accent, and it behaves like a single work-lamp: one clearly dominant amber element per fold. If a screen has amber in three competing places, dim two of them.

rules.name: The Amber-Never-As-Body-Text Rule
rules.body: #E8901C is a fill and a marker, not a text color on light surfaces. Amber-toned words on white/cream must use Amber Ink #7A4708. Enforced in site-polish.css.

rules.name: The Sentence-Case Rule
rules.body: Headings are sentence case. No Title Case, no ALL-CAPS headlines, no exclamation marks. Caps are reserved for short mono labels only.

rules.name: The Mono-Is-Annotation Rule
rules.body: IBM Plex Mono is for callouts that report — labels, tags, HUD readouts, code. Never set a sentence of marketing prose in mono.

rules.name: The Glow-Signals-Life Rule
rules.body: Slate shadow = elevation. Amber glow = live data or focus. Never use amber glow to fake depth, and never use a slate drop-shadow on the dark chrome.

dos: Do keep amber to one dominant element per fold (The One Light Rule).
Do use Amber Ink #7A4708 for any amber-colored text on light surfaces; verify body text ≥4.5:1, large text ≥3:1 (WCAG 2.1 AA).
Do write headings in sentence case with no exclamation marks.
Do reserve IBM Plex Mono for labels, tags, and HUD readouts — annotation, not prose.
Do convey depth on dark chrome with tonal Ink plates, and reserve amber glow for live-data signal.
Do keep content visible by default and animate only already-visible elements; ship a reduced-motion path.
Do test headings in EN, SL, and HR at every breakpoint — the longest translation is part of the design.
donts: Don't ship the generic SaaS template: no cream/gradient-mesh backgrounds, hero-metric card templates, identical icon-card grids, purple gradient buttons, or gradient text.
Don't drift into sterile corporate blue: no stock-photo handshakes, no faceless navy-and-gray walls of body copy.
Don't use #E8901C as body text on light surfaces (fails AA) — use Amber Ink.
Don't set the display heading above 82px or letter-spacing tighter than −0.04em.
Don't add a second competing accent color; blueprint navy is for links/info only.
Don't gate content visibility on a scroll/reveal class — a suspended timeline would strand it hidden.
Don't use amber glow to fake elevation, or slate drop-shadows on the dark chrome.
